---
title: "Week 0 - Basic R Solutions"
output: html_document
---

## Assignment Solutions

1. Use the METABRIC dataset to answer the following questions:
    - How many unique patients?
    - How many older than 75 at diagnosis?
    - Earliest and oldest ages of diagnosis?
    - Chemotherapy and Radiotherapy counts?
    - Less than three mutations?

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# Load data
metabric <- read_csv("../data/metabric_clinical_and_expression_data.csv")

# For how many unique patients we have data available?
n_unique <- n_distinct(metabric$Patient_ID)
cat("The number of unique patients is", n_unique, "\n")

# How many patients were older than 75 when diagnosed?
older_75 <- metabric %>% filter(Age_at_diagnosis > 75) %>% nrow()
cat("Number of patients older than 75:", older_75, "\n")

# Earliest and oldest ages of diagnosis?
age_range <- range(metabric$Age_at_diagnosis, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Age range:", age_range[1], "to", age_range[2], "\n")

# Chemo and Radio?
both_treatments <- metabric %>%
  filter(Chemotherapy == "YES" & Radiotherapy == "YES") %>%
  nrow()
cat("Patients with both treatments:", both_treatments, "\n")

# Less than three mutations?
less_3_muts <- metabric %>% filter(Mutation_count < 3) %>% nrow()
cat("Patients with < 3 mutations:", less_3_muts, "\n")
```

2. Protein sequence analysis function.

```{r}
proteins <- c(
    "MEAGPSGAAAGAYLPPLQQ",
    "VFQAPRRPGIGTVGKPIKLLANYFEVDIPK",
    "IDVYHY",
    "EVDIKPDKCPRRVNREVV",
    "EYMVQHFKPQIFGDRKPVYDGKKNIYTVTALPIGNER"
)

find_abundant_aa <- function(protein) {
    if (nchar(protein) < 10) {
        warning("Protein is < 10 aa long")
    }

    # Split string into characters and create a table
    aa_counts <- table(strsplit(protein, "")[[1]])

    # Find max
    most_abundant <- names(aa_counts)[which.max(aa_counts)]
    return(most_abundant)
}

for (p in proteins) {
    cat("Protein:", p, "\n")
    cat("Most abundant AA:", find_abundant_aa(p), "\n\n")
}
```

3. Debugging the capital city function.

```{r}
capital_cities <- list(
    "Sweden" = "Stockholm",
    "UK" = "London",
    "USA" = "Washington DC"
)

# R version with correct argument order (defaults last)
find_capital_city <- function(country, verbose = TRUE) {
    if (country %in% names(capital_cities)) {
        city <- capital_cities[[country]]
        if (verbose) cat("Capital city located\n")
    } else {
        city <- "CAPITAL CITY NOT FOUND"
    }
    return(city)
}

countries <- c("USA", "UK", "Sweden", "Belgium")
for (c in countries) {
    print(find_capital_city(c))
}
```

4. "Imagine" lyrics word frequency.

```{r}
find_most_frequent_word <- function(file_path, exclude_words = c()) {
    # Read and clean text
    lyrics <- read_lines(file_path) %>%
        str_to_lower() %>%
        str_replace_all("[[:punct:]]", "") %>%
        str_split(" ") %>%
        unlist() %>%
        .[. != ""]

    # Filter
    if (length(exclude_words) > 0) {
        lyrics <- lyrics[!(lyrics %in% str_to_lower(exclude_words))]
    }

    # Count
    word_freq <- table(lyrics)
    return(names(word_freq)[which.max(word_freq)])
}

cat("Most frequent word:", find_most_frequent_word("../data/imagine_lyrics.txt"), "\n")
cat("Most frequent (filtered):", find_most_frequent_word("../data/imagine_lyrics.txt", c("the", "a", "and")), "\n")
```
